authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Rule (34 CFR Part 99)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines student-record access and disclosure limits that directly gate institution reporting outputs.

authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Privacy and Disclosure Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education - Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Operational FERPA interpretation for role-based record handling used in consent and release validation.

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR Part 46 (Common Rule)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Sets protections for study participants and escalation requirements when study-linked data enters disclosures.

authoritative_sources.title: OHRP Informed Consent and IRB Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary guidance on informed consent and IRB state changes that can alter report permissions.

authoritative_sources.title: Uniform Guidance (2 CFR Part 200)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Sets federal stewardship and disclosure obligations for grant-linked institutional outputs.

authoritative_sources.title: IPEDS Program and Enrollment Data
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Public reporting taxonomy and definitions used for official education program and enrollment framing.

authoritative_sources.title: IPEDS Data Collection and Definitions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Public definitions and data-collection conventions used by institutions for official educational reporting.

authoritative_sources.title: IMS OneRoster
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Interoperable learner/course enrollment standard used by institution reporting pipelines.

authoritative_sources.title: CHEA Accreditation Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Accreditation governance reference for escalation boundaries and quality context in education reporting.

workflow_stages.name: Source and consent collection
workflow_stages.description: Validate consent, student/faculty/study context, and source retrieval baseline before any metric computation.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Metric definition and data normalization
workflow_stages.description: Resolve metric naming, numerator/denominator expectations, and record alignment across reporting systems.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references
Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Disclosure-risk and conflict control
workflow_stages.description: Detect stale, conflicting, or incomplete source states and escalate prior to publication-level outputs.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Report assembly and submission logic
workflow_stages.description: Assemble disclosure-ready artifacts with evidence ties, caveats, and owner-routing notes.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Ethics and adjacent-authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate grading, instructional judgment, ethics, or formal approval shifts instead of resolving autonomously.

allowed: Validate metric and record sufficiency for reporting outputs.
Prepare submission and disclosure packages with explicit caveats and confidence limits.
Escalate ethics/IRB or approval-based outcomes to adjacent owners.
escalate: Missing FERPA, consent, or assessment evidence
Unresolved data staleness, source conflicts, or accreditation-policy changes
Requests that require graded/instructional judgment or policy-override authority
refuse: Any request to publish causal claims without evidence provenance.
Closure without complete disclosure and approval controls.

domain_failure_modes: Publication attempts with missing FERPA/consent coverage
Reporting outputs built from stale or conflicting source snapshots
Unreconciled metric definitions used across tenant and external reporting references
Attempt to resolve ethics/IRB or accreditation exceptions without routeable ownership
Disclosure package includes unsupported causal claims or policy assertions

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Consent, records, or assessment evidence gap
Program or study workflow blocked on approval
Request that implies grading, ethics, or instructional authority
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Submission inputs are incomplete or conflicting
Submission inputs are blocked or change after ethics/approval update

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: FERPA role hierarchy vs tenant role model
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Tenant policy and FERPA role mapping must be supplied before resolving final release authority in mixed-policy institutions.
Tenant must nominate the source-of-truth for IRB/ethics status when SIS, LMS, and research-admin systems disagree.

notes: Public source classes are sufficient for baseline operation; tenant policies for metric dictionaries, retention windows, and approval maps remain required inputs at runtime.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because all remaining ambiguities are represented as tenant-adaptable, non-blocking assumptions.
